title: Find missing length when given area of a triangle
description: Find the base or height of a triangle when given the other and the area.
topic_path: Formula for area of a triangle
Find missing length when given area of a triangle

assessment_items.question_content:
The triangle shown below has an area of $20$ units$^2$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the missing length.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints:
We can relate the length $x$ to the area of the triangle with this formula.

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
20&= \dfrac12 \cdot 8 \cdot x \\\\
20&= 4x \\\\
\dfrac{20}{\blueD{4}}&= \dfrac{4x}{\blueD{4}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 4}}\\\\
\dfrac{20}{\blueD{4}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{4}x}{\blueD{\cancel{4}}}\\\\
x &=\dfrac{20}{\blueD{4}}\\\\
x &=5\end{align}$

The answer:

$x = 5$ units

options.alt: A right triangle. The base is labeled 8. The height is labeled x.

assessment_items.question_content:
The triangle shown below has an area of $18$ units$^2$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the missing length.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints:
We can relate the length $x$ to the area of the triangle with this formula.

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
18&= \dfrac12 \cdot 6 \cdot x \\\\
18&= 3x \\\\
\dfrac{18}{\blueD{3}}&= \dfrac{3x}{\blueD{3}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 3}}\\\\
\dfrac{18}{\blueD{3}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{3}x}{\blueD{\cancel{3}}}\\\\
x &=\dfrac{18}{\blueD{3}}\\\\
x &=6\end{align}$

The answer:

$x = 6$ units

options.alt: A right triangle. The base is labeled 6. The height is labeled x.

assessment_items.question_content:
The triangle shown below has an area of $4$ units$^2$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the missing length.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints:
We can relate the length $x$ to the area of the triangle with this formula.

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
4&= \dfrac12 \cdot x \cdot 4 \\\\
4&= 2x \\\\
\dfrac{4}{\blueD{2}}&= \dfrac{2x}{\blueD{2}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 2}}\\\\
\dfrac{4}{\blueD{2}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{2}x}{\blueD{\cancel{2}}}\\\\
x &=\dfrac{4}{\blueD{2}}\\\\
x &=2\end{align}$

The answer:

$x = 2$ units

options.alt: A triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base from the base to the opposite vertex. The dashed line creates two right triangles. An arrow shows the length of the base is labeled x. The dashed line is labeled 4.

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\

assessment_items.question_content:

hints:
We can relate the length $x$ to the area of the triangle with this formula.

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
50&= \dfrac12 \cdot 10 \cdot x \\\\
50&= 5x \\\\
\dfrac{50}{\blueD{5}}&= \dfrac{5x}{\blueD{5}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 5}}\\\\
\dfrac{50}{\blueD{5}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{5}x}{\blueD{\cancel{5}}}\\\\
x &=\dfrac{50}{\blueD{5}}\\\\
x &=10\end{align}$

The answer:

$x = 10$ units

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
12&= \dfrac12 \cdot 6 \cdot x \\\\
12&= 3x \\\\
\dfrac{12}{\blueD{3}}&= \dfrac{3x}{\blueD{3}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 3}}\\\\
\dfrac{12}{\blueD{3}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{3}x}{\blueD{\cancel{3}}}\\\\
x &=\dfrac{12}{\blueD{3}}\\\\

[[☃ image 1]]

hints:
We can relate the length $x$ to the area of the triangle with this formula.

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
10&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot x \\\\
10&= 2x \\\\
\dfrac{10}{\blueD{2}}&= \dfrac{2x}{\blueD{2}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 2}}\\\\
\dfrac{10}{\blueD{2}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{2}x}{\blueD{\cancel{2}}}\\\\

assessment_items.question_content:
The triangle shown below has an area of $5$ units$^2$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the missing length.**



$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints:
We can relate the length $x$ to the area of the triangle with this formula.

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
5&= \dfrac12 \cdot x \cdot 5 \\\\
5&= 2.5x \\\\
\dfrac{5}{\blueD{2.5}}&= \dfrac{2.5x}{\blueD{2.5}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 2.5}}\\\\
\dfrac{5}{\blueD{2.5}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{2.5}x}{\blueD{\cancel{2.5}}}\\\\
x &=\dfrac{5}{\blueD{2.5}}\\\\
x &=2\end{align}$

The answer:

$x = 2$ units

options.alt: A scalene triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base from the base to the opposite vertex. The base is labeled x. The height is labeled 5.

assessment_items.question_content:
The triangle shown below has an area of $16$ units$^2$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the missing length.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints:
We can relate the length $x$ to the area of the triangle with this formula.

$\text{Area of a triangle} = \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$

Let's fill in the values we know and solve for $x$.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &= \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
16&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot x \\\\
16&= 2x \\\\
\dfrac{16}{\blueD{2}}&= \dfrac{2x}{\blueD{2}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 2}}\\\\
\dfrac{16}{\blueD{2}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{2}x}{\blueD{\cancel{2}}}\\\\
x &=\dfrac{16}{\blueD{2}}\\\\
x &=8\end{align}$

The answer:

$x = 8$ units

options.alt: A scalene triangle. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base from the base to the opposite vertex. The base is labeled 4. The height is labeled x.
